message:
test(conformance): drop XFAIL skiplist from live core_strings oracle diff

live_redis_core_strings_matches_runtime was running through
run_live_redis_diff_for_cases with a hand-maintained XFAIL list of
13 absolute-time TTL observation cases (pexpiretime_abskey,
expiretime_abskey, pttl_whenkey_after_expireat, pexpireat_whenkey,
pttl_whenkey_after_pexpireat, expiretime_whenkey,
getex_verify_ttl_after_exat, getex_with_pxat,
getex_verify_pttl_after_pxat, getex_with_persist,
getex_verify_ttl_after_persist, set_exat_verify_ttl,
set_pxat_verify_pttl) plus an implicit exclusion of INCRBYFLOAT and
HINCRBYFLOAT non-terminating-decimal cases by way of duplicate case
names in the fixture.

The skiplist was load-bearing only under STRICT; in the default
tolerant mode, run_live_diff_tolerant already downgrades these
divergences to stderr without failing the test. The names-based filter
was also silently ineffective for the float cases because the fixture
has duplicate case names and per-name filtering cannot distinguish
them — so the XFAIL was accurate for the TTL set but a no-op for the
float set, leaving the test in an inconsistent "documented XFAIL"
posture that did not match its actual runtime behavior.

Drop run_live_redis_diff_for_cases + fixture load + XFAIL vec in favor
of a straight run_live_redis_diff call. The tolerant-mode wrapper
remains and still reports divergences to stderr; tracking of the two
known diverging families stays on br-frankenredis-7rp6.

Rewrite the doc comment to describe both divergence classes and to
call out the duplicate-name limitation, so the next reader understands
why the test intentionally stays tolerant rather than gaining a
per-case XFAIL skiplist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
